name: Livagen
subtitle: KEDA Tetrapeptide | Liver Bioregulator
overview: Livagen is a Khavinson bioregulator tetrapeptide (KEDA) with significant hepatoprotective and immunomodulatory properties. Structurally similar to Epitalon, it normalizes immune and antioxidant status while restoring liver function in hepatitis conditions. Livagen is best known for its ability to decondense chromatin, activating silent genes including ribosomal genes to boost protein synthesis and cellular activity. Maximum protective effects occur during aging.
mechanism: Livagen works through direct effects on DNA structure and function. It decondenses chromatin (tightly packed DNA), increasing expression of certain genes and improving the 'youthful' profile of cells. The peptide activates multiple genes in lymphocytes, including previously silent ribosomal genes, boosting protein synthesis and cell activity. It exhibits hepatoprotective effects through normalizing immune and antioxidant status in liver tissue.
keyBenefits: Boosts protein synthesis and cell activity

items.name: Hepatoprotection
items.description: Protects liver tissue through normalizing immune and antioxidant status.

items.name: Hepatitis Support
items.description: Restores liver functions in both acute and chronic hepatitis models.

items.name: Liver Fibrosis
items.description: Shows protective properties in liver fibroid induration models.

items.name: Chromatin Remodeling
items.description: Decondenses chromatin to restore youthful gene expression patterns.

items.name: Age-Related Liver Decline
items.description: Maximum hepatoprotective effects occur during aging.

items.name: Gene Activation
items.description: Activates silent genes including ribosomal genes for improved protein synthesis.

items.name: Immunomodulation
items.description: Normalizes immune function alongside liver protective effects.

deliveryMethods.overview: Available in capsule form for oral administration. Short peptides can be absorbed orally and reach target tissues. Typical protocol involves 10-20 day cycles, often repeated 2-3 times per year.
Injectable form available for direct administration.

interactions.notes: Structurally similar; often combined in comprehensive anti-aging protocols.
Both support liver function and antioxidant status.
Complementary liver bioregulators with different sequences.
Different organ targets; can be combined in bioregulator protocols.

timeline.effects: Chromatin remodeling and gene expression changes begin
Effects persist due to epigenetic changes

references.title: Influence of KEDA Tetrapeptide on Organism Physiological Function
references.keyFindings: KEDA exhibits hepatoprotective, immunomodulatory, and geroprotective properties with maximum effects during aging.

references.title: Effects of Livagen Peptide on Chromatin Activation in Lymphocytes from Old People
references.journal: Bulletin of Experimental Biology and Medicine
references.keyFindings: Livagen activates chromatin decondensation and multiple genes in aged lymphocytes.

references.title: Hepatoprotective Properties of KEDA in Hepatitis Models

references.title: Khavinson Peptide Bioregulators
